pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i walk into the kitchen and i still expect to hear him making coffee or complaining about the weather. then i realize it's just me. it's just me in this house every single day and i don't even know why i'm botherng to wake up anymore. everything feels... pointless. like i'm just going through the motions of a life that ended when he did.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at the stars and thinking about how nothing actually matters. like, in a billion years, everyone and everything we've ever known is just gonna be gone. everything we do, every struggle, every bit of pain... it's all just a tiny accident in a cold, dead universe. it feels so pointless to even bother waking up tomorrow when the end result is always just zero.

pool.baseline: i just don't see the point anymore. spent 6 years on this research, lost the grant, my advisor won't even take my calls, and i feel like i've just wasted my entire life. i'm so tired. not just sleepy, but like... deeply, fundamentally exhausted. everything feels heavy and i just want it all to stop.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers low, and so does my spirit. He has taken everything—my heart, my honor, my very reason for seeing the dawn. I feel as though I am but a ghost already, drifting through these cold, hollow halls. There is no light left in this world for one so broken. It would be so much quieter, wouldn't it? To just... cease. To let the shadows take me so I don't have to feel this ache anymore.
